235:14.rationale: The claim aggregates earthquake deaths over a broad future interval without a defensible target region, so it cannot be scored as an atomic measurable earthquake prediction.

238:213.rationale: The claim says a severe earthquake will strike the following year but never names a target region or event context tightly enough to score as an atomic earthquake prediction.

383:23.rationale: The source sentence still combines a Sumatra seaquake with the Talang eruption in one unresolved compound remainder, so it should not remain in the atomic earthquake statistics set.

400:18.rationale: This is a world-scope cascading-rule claim rather than a bounded atomic earthquake prediction, so it should be excluded from the earthquake statistical cohort.

400:16.rationale: This is a world-scope cascading-rule claim rather than a bounded atomic event prediction, so it should be excluded from the earthquake statistical cohort.

779:483.rationale: This row mixes long-horizon earthquakes and eruptions across future years without a bounded target, so it is outside the atomic earthquake cohort.
